title: Harden Agent Workspace outcomes and enforce evidence-bound adoption
goal: Correct the live observer and optimizer defects, require an explicit route decision before nontrivial code work, record a bounded outcome after every workspace, and validate the current Agent Workspace on one real medium-sized code ball without making it the default for trivial work.

acceptance.assertion: Before every nontrivial code ball, the operator records the typed route recommendation, actual route, input facts and any justified deviation. Trivial work is narrowly defined as at most one file, at most fifteen expected minutes, low novelty, no risk flag, no connector instability and no parallel work.

Risky, parallel, connector-unstable or salvage-relevant work uses a full workspace unless a recorded feasibility blocker prevents it. Small low-risk work remains direct or an isolated worktree; the route tool recommends but never grants merge, deployment or external-agent authority.

Legacy receipts classify missing executables and Python modules as environment/toolchain failures, never as semantic test failures; already_succeeded is a success state rather than a failure class; recoverable writer head, base and diff identity are reported from immutable receipts or collection metadata.

Observer closeout accepts only explicitly supplied or independently read evidence for PR/integration truth, Bureau reconciliation, lease release, checkout archive/cleanup and final summary. Completed items are not left unresolved, but unknown items remain unknown and no automatic mutation authority is added.

The optimizer excludes success states, legacy event-log absence and unknown closeout from failure counts. A structural proposal requires the same actionable failure class in at least two independent workspaces and reports sample size, expected benefit, regression risk and a normal reviewed validation plan.

Every collected or closed workspace emits or references a bounded outcome record containing recommendation and actual route, first-pass role results, retries, elapsed time, tool calls, frozen result identity, integration or salvage outcome and external closeout completion. Missing fields fail closed instead of being silently inferred.

After the corrections are merged and deployed, one real medium-sized code ball is routed through the current workspace. Its result records useful code retained, first-pass test/review outcome, overhead, failures caught, salvage value and comparison against an explicitly selected direct-work baseline without claiming general productivity from one case.

Regression fixtures reproduce both existing workspace runs and prove the corrected classifications and optimizer silence. Focused/full tests and required CI pass; the merged runtime is deployed; the pilot is collected, externally reconciled and closed with exact hashes.

metadata.source: Live Agent Workspace usefulness and adoption audit
metadata.blocking_reason: The Grabowski repository is held by the foreign Consumer Surface v2 ball and T036 is stacked on its head. The one-active-ball rule and exact lease ownership prohibit starting this implementation now.
metadata.queue_decision: Registered but not queued while blocked; no second Grabowski ball is started.
scope_hint: runtime and publication contracts only when generated output requires them
does_not_establish: automatic workspace creation for trivial work
observer access to unrestricted repository or home-directory content
general productivity improvement from one pilot
permission to alter the active Consumer Surface v2 or T036 worktrees
